provisions.content: This Order may be cited as the Visiting Forces (Application of Enactments) Order, and shall be deemed to have come into operation on the 20th January, 1964 in the case of the Visiting Forces of the United Kingdom and Northern Ireland; on the 31st March, 1964 in the case of the visiting forces of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and on the twenty-sixth day of June, 1964 in the case of visiting forces of Ethiopia.

The United Kingdom and Northern Ireland forces, the forces of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, and the Ethiopia forces, their members and the property used or to be used for the purposes of such forces and members are hereby exempt from or, as the case may be, entitled to benefit under the provisions of the enactments specified in the Schedule hereto to the extent to which such forces, members or property would be exempt therefrom or entitled to benefit thereunder if such forces were a part of the Tanzania Peoples Defence Forces. Schedule Cap. or Act No. Title Provision Cap. 168 The Road Traffic Act Part 1 (dealing with registration and licensing of vehicles and drivers) Cap. 169 The Motor Vehicles Insurance Act Sections 4 and 14 Cap. 45 The Explosives Act The whole Act Cap. 225 The Petroleum Rules, 1951 Rule 4(2) Cap. 403 The Customs (Management and Tariff) Act Section 199 and relevant items of the Fourth Schedule Cap. 192 National Defence Act Section 171 History of this document 31 July 2002 this version Consolidation 26 June 1964 Commenced Note: As it relates to the visiting forces of Ethiopia 31 March 1964 Commenced Note: As it relates to the visiting forces of the Federal Republic of Nigeria 20 January 1964 Commenced Note: As it relates to the visiting forces of the the United Kingdom and Northern Ireland Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all. Free Access to the law supports the rule of law and access to justice. TanzLII partners with African Legal Information Institute and Laws.Africa , who provide it with technical support.
